content.problemStatement:
Chef loves dealing with binomial coefficients. Today, he came across an interesting problem, but he is currently busy in the kitchen. Can you help Chef solve this problem?

You are given three positive integers NNN, ppp and rrr. Calculate the sum
S=∑i=0N(pir) .S = \sum\limits_{i=0}^{N} \binom{p^i}{r} \,.S=i=0∑N​(rpi​).

Since this sum could be very large, calculate it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, ppp and rrr.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the sum SSS modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
